dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8195 IH: Responsible Cormorant Management and Control Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Walberg introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of the Interior to develop regional management frameworks for the take of double-crested cormorants.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Responsible Cormorant Management and Control Act of 2026.

section.header: Regional management frameworks for take of double-crested cormorants

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this section, the Secretary, in coordination with the applicable Regional Flyway Council, shall develop a regional management framework for the take of double-crested cormorants for each region covered by a Regional Flyway Council based on existing information.
Each regional management framework developed under subsection (a) shall—

paragraph.text: ensure the breeding population of double-crested cormorants is maintained at a sustainable level—

subparagraph.text: in accordance with the Migratory Bird Treaty Act (16 U.S.C. 703 et seq.); and
beginning with the first update required by subsection (e), as informed by the most recent survey carried out under subsection (d);

paragraph.text: include, with respect to double-crested cormorants—
identify entities that may take double-crested cormorants, which shall include—

subparagraph.text: entities licensed or otherwise authorized in a manner the Secretary determines appropriate, which shall include—

clause.text: State licensed or otherwise authorized hunters;

subparagraph.text: States and Indian Tribes to take specific management actions with respect to double-crested cormorants in accordance with the regional management framework; and
the implementation of management actions identified under subsection (c); and

paragraph.text: account for the effects of an overabundant population of double-crested cormorants on—

subparagraph.text: species listed as threatened species or endangered species under section 4 of the Endangered Species Act of 1973 (16 U.S.C. 1533).

subsection.header: Incorporation of National Wildlife Refuge System
subsection.text: In developing each regional management framework under subsection (a), the Secretary, in cooperation with the heads of State and Tribal game and fish agencies and the applicable Regional Flyway Council, shall identify appropriate management actions that can be taken within units of the National Wildlife Refuge System to meet management objectives of States, Indian Tribes, the Regional Flyway Councils, and such units with respect to double-crested cormorants while fulfilling the primary purposes of such units.

Not later than 5 years after the date of the enactment of this section and every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary, in coordination with the Regional Flyway Councils, shall carry out a survey of the population of double-crested cormorants.

paragraph.text: Not later than 5 years after the date of the enactment of this section and every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary, in coordination with the applicable Regional Flyway Council, shall review and update each regional management framework developed under subsection (a).
In reviewing and updating a regional management framework under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall incorporate the results of the most recent survey carried out under subsection (d).
The term double-crested cormorant means the species Nannopterum auritum.
The term Regional Flyway Council means—
The term Indian Tribe has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304).
The term lake manager means a person that is licensed or otherwise authorized by a State regulatory agency to manage a private lake.
The term migratory bird has the meaning given the term migratory birds in section 11 of the Migratory Bird Conservation Act (16 U.S.C. 715j).
The term pond manager means a person that is licensed or otherwise authorized by a State regulatory agency to manage a private pond.
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.
The term take has the meaning given the term in section 10.12 of title 50, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section).
